summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined several specific developments in small UAS activity. A Ukrainian FPV drone struck a US military base in Iraq, marking a first-ever claimed attack by an Iran-backed militia, with video evidence released. In Donetsk Region, Ukrainian FPV drones have been targeting Russian logistics up to 50 km behind the front line, and Ukrainian engineers have developed the "Chipa" net gun to physically snare FPV drones when other air defenses fail. Russia has tested a white "penguin" suit with black spots in Ukraine, but at least two soldiers wearing it were quickly located and neutralized by FPV drones in snowy terrain. Additionally, Russia is rushing to expand its drone force to 101,000 troops by April, while Ukraine reported hitting 105,200 targets in February, and Ukrainian strikes have reduced Russian FPV drone activity by 18% in the same month.
